tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: With only ten percent of the assignment remaining and no other immediate demands, I can finalize this submission to secure the academic credit before the deadline.
I missed the deadline despite being 95% complete, likely due to over-planning rather than rushing. The academic loss is now unavoidable, and my stress has risen accordingly.
The assignment is nearly complete, but my stress remains high from the missed deadline. I must finish this submission to salvage the academic credit despite the setback.
I've reached 99% completion on the assignment, yet the missed deadline still looms. I will submit immediately to secure whatever partial credit remains.

desires: Secure the maximum possible partial credit from this submission to minimize the academic loss caused by my over-planning.
Avoid any further interpersonal friction or awkward explanations with the professor regarding the missed deadline.
Prevent immediate physical exhaustion and protect my sleep discipline for tomorrow despite the current stress spike.
Resolve the lingering task completion loop to eliminate the cognitive drain of an unfinished assignment.

goals.description: Execute final submission of the graded assignment immediately to lock in partial credit.
goals.rationale: The 'achievement' axis demands maximizing academic ROI; with progress at 98.7%, abandoning now yields zero value, while finishing secures a partial grade. The 'deliberate' nature confirms that rushing the final click is more efficient than prolonging the cognitive loop. This resolves the task completion anxiety.

goals.description: Maintain silence and submit without adding any explanatory text or apology to the professor.
goals.rationale: Consistent with the 'avoidant' conflict style, introducing communication now creates unnecessary interpersonal friction for no tangible benefit. The transaction is complete upon submission; further engagement risks energy depletion and social awkwardness.

goals.description: Immediately cease all work and initiate wind-down routine to protect sleep discipline.
goals.rationale: Despite the stress spike, my 'sleep discipline' (8/10) and 'long planning horizon' require protecting tomorrow's focus capacity. Continuing to optimize after submission yields diminishing returns and risks physical exhaustion, which would compromise future achievement potential.

goals.description: Defer note tidying and administrative tasks until a dedicated block later this week.
goals.rationale: These are low-value activities relative to the primary academic objective. Deferring them aligns with the 'deliberate' approach of batching low-stakes tasks rather than fragmenting attention during high-stress transitions.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances the high-priority goal of locking in partial credit (achievement axis). With progress at 99%, a brief final push avoids future regret without triggering conflict or depleting energy needed for tomorrow's sleep discipline.

goals.description: Maintain silence and avoid non-essential communication until submission is confirmed.
goals.rationale: As an 'avoidant' individual, I must minimize interpersonal friction. Engaging with teammates or professors now could introduce unnecessary stress or delay. Staying focused ensures a polished delivery that preserves my reputation for reliability without social cost.

goals.description: Defer low-priority note tidying until after the submission is complete.
goals.rationale: Given my 'deliberate' nature and limited time window, spreading focus across non-urgent tasks would dilute the quality of the primary academic deliverable. This aligns with my preference for long-term planning over immediate, trivial gains.

desires: Secure the academic credit by submitting the assignment before the 13:30 deadline to maximize long-term grade outcomes.

Preserve physical stamina and minimize stress accumulation to maintain focus capacity for future tasks.
